Sensex, Nifty close in green as easing Israel-Iran tensions lift sentimentThe BSE Sensex gained 394.50 points, or 0.54%, to close at 73,918.76. The NSE Nifty50 rose 119.10 points, or 0.52%, to settle at 23,242.10.

Why the government cut Ujjwala LPG subsidy from nine to four cylinders a yearThe government has said the cut in subsidised Ujjwala LPG refills was driven by alleged diversion of cylinders for commercial use.

OpenAI files confidential IPO, eyes trillion-dollar valuation in AI boomThe company announced on Monday that it had submitted confidential paperwork for a stock market listing. However, OpenAI did not reveal the size of the offering, its expected valuation, or when it plans to go public.

Ujjwala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

Air India may introduce 'no meals' tickets on domestic flights to cut faresAir India is considering a no-meals ticket category for domestic passengers. The plan could begin on select short routes and lower fares for flyers who skip onboard food.

Builder delayed possession? Here's what homebuyers can doBuilder delayed possession of your flat? Learn your rights under RERA, when you can seek compensation or a refund, whether you must continue paying EMI, and what legal and financial options homebuyers have when projects are delayed.

Oil prices cannot stay this high for long, says Oil Minister Hardeep Singh PuriWhile fuel prices remain elevated for now, Puri's comments suggest that policymakers expect the pressure to ease over time.

Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

entertainment.headline: Kusha Kapila, Dolly Singh join outrage against Pranit More over lead outrage Content creators Kusha Kapila and Dolly Singh called out comedian Pranit More over a viral crowd-work video, slamming his handling of a misogynistic audience remark.

Shalin Bhanot reacts to remarriage rumours with Dalljiet Kaur, demands apology Shalin Bhanot has reacted sharply to viral claims about remarrying his former wife Dalljiet Kaur. The actor has issued a warning and demanded immediate action over the reports.

I lost my beautiful sister today: Sohail Khan mourns Kumod Raney's death Sohail Khan mourned the death of longtime family friend Kumod Raney with an emotional social media post. His tribute drew condolences from several celebrities and fans after Raney's death on June 9.

Veteran Tamil director Bharathiraja dies due to prolonged illness at 85 in Chennai Veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja died in Chennai on Monday after prolonged ill health. He was 85. More details regarding the funeral are awaited.

Baahubali to Peddi: How pan-India cinema exposed South cinema's gender problem A scene in Peddi showing Ram Charan kissing Janhvi Kapoor without consent triggered a national backlash. Would Janhvi Kapoor's objectification in a Telugu film have become a national debate if India hadn't embraced the pan-India cinema culture?

Lock Upp Season 2: Is Kangana Ranaut returning as host? Here's what we know Lock Upp is making a comeback after four years with a new OTT home and a fresh season. But will Kangana Ranaut return as host? Here's everything revealed so far about the release date, streaming platform and hosting rumours.

Katy Perry goes full heart-eyes for Justin Trudeau, calls him the 'love of my life' Katy Perry isn't holding back anymore. The singer publicly called Justin Trudeau the "love of my life" at her Tribeca premiere, revealing how the former Canadian Prime Minister makes her feel "whole" after a tumultuous year.

Raj Kundra helps female airport staff, praised for respectful demeanour Entrepreneur-turned-actor Raj Kundra reportedly stepped in at Mumbai airport after a passenger allegedly abused and intimidated a female staff member. The staffer later thanked him for standing up for her and helping defuse the situation.

Singer Sapna Choudhary gets protection order, husband barred from contacting her Singer Sapna Choudhary has been granted interim protection by the Dwarka Mahila Court ahead of the premiere of her upcoming film Momacu. The court has restrained her husband from contacting or approaching her until the next hearing.

Elle trailer: The Legally Blonde prequel brings pink power to high school The first trailer for Elle introduces Elle Woods, played by Lexi Minetree, as a pink-loving teenager navigating high school in 1995. It sets up the friendships, romance and family bonds that shape the woman audiences later meet at Harvard.

Enola Holmes 3 trailer: Millie Bobby Brown races to save kidnapped Sherlock The trailer for Enola Holmes 3 promises the young detective's most dangerous case yet as she sets out to rescue her kidnapped brother, Sherlock Holmes. Starring Millie Bobby Brown and Henry Cavill, the Netflix film arrives on July 1, 2026.

It took every cell in my body to undo the bullying and prejudice: Kangana Ranaut Kangana Ranaut opened up about overcoming prejudice and finding strength through difficult phases of her life. The actor said bravery comes in many forms, from showing up during personal struggles to confronting criticism and self-doubt.

Actor Akansha Ranjan Kapoor, filmmaker Sharan Sharma set for a July wedding Actor Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and director Sharan Sharma are reportedly set to marry in Mumbai on July 11. The couple is planning a private celebration with close family and friends, followed by a reception on July 12.

Bigg Boss 20: Salman Khan to begin shoot in September, auditions to begin soon Salman Khan is set to begin filming Bigg Boss 20 on September 21 while juggling his film commitments. The move sets the stage for auditions, casting talks and fresh format details in the coming weeks.

Amid Peddi row, Kangana Ranaut says women actors can flag problematic scenes Kangana Ranaut has weighed in on the controversy surrounding Peddi, saying women actors should voice their concerns if a scene feels uncomfortable or problematic.

Jacob Elordi snaps back during a fan encounter in Japan: Don't touch me A video of actor Jacob Elordi confronting a fan who touched him during a photo request in Japan has gone viral on social media. The incident comes as buzz builds around his upcoming film The Dog Stars and dating rumours with model Kendall Jenner.

Katrina Kaif eyes OTT projects for her comeback: Sources Actor Katrina Kaif has reportedly resumed reading scripts and is considering OTT projects for her return to acting. The move could mark her digital debut, with the actor said to be looking for a role that aligns with her priorities after embracing motherhood.

Maa Behen's Dharna Durga tops IMDB popularity list, beats Madhuri and Triptii Dharna Durga finished ahead of Madhuri Dixit and Triptii Dimri on this week's IMDb Popular Indian Celebrities list. The ranking put the spotlight on Maa Behen as the Netflix film continued to draw attention for its humour and subversion of patriarchal tropes.

Edgy, neurotic, suicidal: Kangana Ranaut breaks down her early career image Actor Kangana Ranaut, in an exclusive interview, revisited the edgy and neurotic roles that shaped her early image. She said each career phase helped her resist typecasting and move towards new characters.

general news.headline: US launches strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter downed in HormuzThe US launched strikes on Iranian air defense and radar systems near the Strait of Hormuz after President Donald Trump said Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter. Explosions were reported across southern Iran, while Tehran denied offensive operations but warned of retaliation.

Political, not legal: Meenakshi Natarajan after Rajya Sabha nomination rejectedMeenakshi Natarajan has accused the BJP of manipulating the Madhya Pradesh Rajya Sabha election after her nomination was rejected over a Telangana court matter. The row has sharpened the political contest before the June 18 election, with Congress and the BJP trading charges over disclosure rules and fairness.

POK on edge, Zojila cheer in India: The tale of two Kashmirs divided by LoCSecurity forces in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (POK) killed at least 27 people even as they did everything possible to foil a protest. Some 100 km to the east, engineers in Jammu and Kashmir celebrated the Zojila Tunnel breakthrough. The two developments show the divergent trajectories taken by two Kashmirs divided by the Line of Control (LoC).

Air India 171 needs answers, not another year of speculationFor the past year, the crash of Air India Flight 171 has divided aviation commentary into two stark camps: cockpit action and technical malfunction. As we approach the one-year anniversary of the tragedy that took place in Ahmedabad, it is time we got some concrete answers.

The Opposition Doesn't Need INDIA 2.0. It Needs Congress 2.0Without a deeper Congress-led political reunification, INDIA 2.0 risks becoming less a revival of the opposition and more a sequel struggling to recreate an old hit.

The men beside me during Peddi explain why Janhvi is hyper-sexualised in the filmI watched Ram Charan's Peddi alone, and the two men seated beside me kept laughing through Janhvi Kapoor's scenes while repeatedly invading my personal space. If this is how some people react in theatres, perhaps it's time we questioned what we're normalising on screen.

How Vijay turned Bigil's Singappenney anthem into Tamil Nadu policyOne of the first major initiatives announced by the Vijay-led TVK government was the creation of the Singappenn Special Task Force, a dedicated unit aimed at strengthening the safety of women and children across the state.

The Supreme Court has reset the moral compass on consensual pre-marital sexThe Supreme Court said a consensual relationship between unmarried adults cannot by itself define character. The observation shifts the conversation from moral judgment to consent, autonomy and personal choice.

Live: US struck water storage tanks in Sirik, cut off supply to locals, claims IranUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East shows no signs of abating as Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) on Wednesday morning said it launched Shahed-136 drones against the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, describing the strike as retaliation for recent US attacks on Iranian infrastructure. The claim followed a warning from Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi that Tehran would respond to US military action. Meanwhile, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said it conducted "self-defence" strikes on Iranian air-defence systems, radar installations and ground-control sites near the Strait of Hormuz after the reported downing of a US Army Apache helicopter. Washington characterised the operation as a proportional response to attacks on US forces and commercial shipping, while Iran portrayed it as further escalation. Both sides have exchanged competing claims over the effectiveness of their strikes, underscoring the fragility of ongoing diplomatic efforts. Separately, Israeli operations in Lebanon continue, with recent air and drone strikes reported in southern Lebanon and near Beirut amid renewed tensions with Hezbollah, further complicating regional stability. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

India freezes Starlink's approval after concerns over use during Iran warStarlink's India entry has reportedly hit a roadblock, with security agencies withholding final clearance over concerns linked to its global operations and the reported use of its terminals during the Iran conflict.

Rs 2.5 lakh Japan school trip costs more than 25 years of Kendriya Vidyalaya feesA viral social media post about a Class 8 student's Rs 2.5 lakh school trip to Japan has revived a familiar debate: when educational experiences start costing more than education itself. The amount is so large that it could cover the annual fees of a Kendriya Vidyalaya student for more than two decades.

Married and anonymous: What 4.5 crore+ Indians seek on 'secret' dating appsMore than 4.5 crore Indians have used discreet dating apps catering to anonymous encounters, open relationships and affairs. A closer look reveals users' motivations, evolving relationship trends and the business of monetising desire.

Behind TMC MPs' rebellion, heat on the ground and an enabler BJPTrinamool Congress MPs are aware of the massive backlash that local party leaders are facing in West Bengal. Public fury, erosion of support and an enabling BJP is reportedly making over 20 TMC MPs ditch Mamata Banerjee and side with the NDA.

African asylum seeker tries to 'behead' man in Belfast, anti-immigration stir eruptsAuthorities have charged a 30-year-old Sudanese asylum seeker with attempted murder after the attack, which left the man fighting for his life and ignited public anger that spilled onto the streets.

Test pilots, seasoned astronauts, surgeon: Meet Nasa's Artemis-III crewNasa has named Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas for Artemis III. Their mission will carry out key docking tests and gather data for future lunar expeditions.

Hero locals step in, beat Belfast knifeman with sticks to stop 'beheading' attackA Sudanese man in his 30s was arrested at the scene on suspicion of attempted murder. Police said they had recovered what is believed to be a kitchen knife and launched a major investigation into the incident.

The growing constellation is seen as strengthening Islamabad's ability to monitor Indian territory and strategic activity.

Writing exams and beating Full Members? Netherlands women eye more at T20 World Cup The Netherlands head into their maiden Women's T20 World Cup believing they can compete with the sport's biggest teams after a historic win over Bangladesh. For a squad balancing studies, jobs, and cricket, the tournament is also a chance to earn franchise opportunities and keep their cricketing dreams alive.

Can India Women end T20 World Cup heartbreak after ODI glory? ICC Women's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur guided India to their maiden ICC title last year. Now, the Indian skipper will be hoping to carry that winning momentum into the Women's T20 World Cup, a trophy that has so far eluded India despite several near misses on the global stage.

Inglis disappointed as Australia suffer first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years Bangladesh beat Australia by 86 runs in the first ODI in Dhaka, with Josh Inglis left frustrated by a flat team performance. The defeat ended Australia's 21-year unbeaten run against Bangladesh in ODIs and handed the hosts a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

Atletico ridicule Real Madrid after rejecting blockbuster offer for Julian Alvarez Real Madrid's attempt to sign Julian Alvarez for a club-record €150 million was met with ridicule from Atletico Madrid, who swiftly rejected the offer and publicly taunted their city rivals.

Serena Williams mulls Wimbledon comeback after winning return to tennis Serena Williams has not ruled out a return to Wimbledon after making a winning comeback to professional tennis nearly four years after her last competitive match.

FIFA World Cup 2026 wonder kids: 5 breakout stars set to explode transfer market FIFA World Cup 2026 breakout stars: From Nico Paz's pristine vision to Endrick's clinical scoring aura, a new generation of fearless prodigies is ready to take centre stage in North America.

Pooja Vastrakar reveals how RCB helped her regain confidence India all-rounder Pooja Vastrakar has credited Royal Challengers Bengaluru for backing her through a difficult injury phase, saying the franchise supported her recovery and focused on her batting while easing her back into bowling.

Alexander Zverev abruptly cuts off interview after domestic violence questions Alexander Zverev finally won his first Grand Slam title at the 2026 French Open, defeating Flavio Cobolli. However, his achievement was quickly overshadowed as post-match discussions shifted to past domestic violence allegations.

England captain Ben Stokes could retire after nightclub controversy Ben Stokes' England future has been thrown into doubt after a nightclub controversy triggered an ECB investigation. Reports suggest the Test captain could lose the captaincy, take a break from cricket, or even retire from international cricket altogether.

From Alana King to Nensi Patel: Indian-origin stars to watch out forIndia head into the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 as one of the title contenders after their ODI World Cup triumph. But beyond Harmanpreet Kaur's side, several Indian-origin players representing other nations are also set to leave their mark.

A World Cup of chaos: Ex-England star blasts hosts USA over visa messIan Wright has labelled the FIFA World Cup 2026 the "World Cup of chaos" amid mounting concerns over visa denials and travel disruptions. The former England star's criticism follows the exclusion of African referee Omar Artan from the tournament.

Pakistan endure Women's T20 World Cup warm-up horror after shock defeat to ScotlandPakistan's preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup suffered another setback as they slumped to a defeat against Scotland. Pakistan's batting faltered, leaving Fatima Sana's side with plenty to address ahead of a daunting group-stage campaign.

Gaikwad proves selectors wrong as India-A win thriller in DambullaIndia A beat Sri Lanka A in an absolute thriller in Dambulla on Tuesday, June 9. Playing their first match in the tri-series, India dragged the match back in the final 3 overs to win by 8 runs.

Indian football crisis nears its end as AIFF and ISL teams agree on club-led modelThe Indian Super League is set to move forward after the AIFF agreed in principle to a two-year club-led model. The breakthrough, backed by the Sports Ministry, clears the path for the 2026-27 season to begin on September 4.

Group B preview: Can West Indies, Sri Lanka surprise the favourites?Women's T20 World Cup: England and New Zealand are widely considered favourites to reach the semifinals, but West Indies and Sri Lanka have the ability to spring surprises. Meanwhile, Ireland and Scotland will be looking to cause a few upsets along the way.

Arjun Tendulkar shines in Mumbai T20 League, gets big dressing room ovationArjun Tendulkar produced a stunning all-round display to power Arcs Andheri to a dominant win over Bandra Blasters in the T20 Mumbai League. The youngster was later rewarded with an emotional dressing-room ovation led by captain Shivam Dube.

Workhorse Mohammed Siraj withdrawn from India's T20I tour of England and IrelandMohammed Siraj has been rested from India's T20Is in Ireland and England under a workload plan. Prasidh Krishna replaces him as India manage resources before a long white-ball season, with Siraj retained for the ODI leg.

FIFA denies tickets to Iran football fans just days ahead of World CupIran's football federation has accused FIFA of pulling its World Cup ticket allocation just days before the tournament begins, leaving thousands of supporters in limbo after many had already booked flights, accommodation and travel plans to follow the national team.

Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya cleared fit for ODIs vs AfghanistanIND vs AFG: Hardik Pandya and Rohit Sharma have been cleared for India's ODI series against Afghanistan. Their return strengthens the batting-bowling balance, while Virat Kohli remains currently sidelined with a hamstring injury.

Footballer detained, referee deported: FIFA World Cup is a mess before kick-offJust days before the beginning of football's greatest spectacle, the FIFA World Cup has run into a myriad of controversies. The latest of those involves Africa's best football referee.

Bangladesh T20 captain reacts to WC boycott: Can play in Pak, then why not India?Litton Das has challenged Bangladesh's official line on pulling out of the T20 World Cup in India over security. His account raises fresh questions about why the call was eventually made.

SL A vs IND A highlights: India beat Sri Lanka by 8 runs in Dambulla thrillerSri Lanka A vs India A highlights: Sri Lanka-A saw their final three wickets go down in the 49th over, as the Ruturaj Gaikwad-led India snatched away an 8-run win over the hosts in some formidable clutch style. 2 wickets and a run out in Arshad Khan's penultimate over marked the end of a thrilling clash in Dambulla, which did not even have a Vaibhav Sooryavanshi show.

No rivals, beyond medals: How yogasana became more than a hunt for gloryAt the first World Yogasana Championship, athletes and coaches from 78 countries competed while often helping one another on and off the mat. The event suggested yogasana's strongest claim is not just medals, but a transnational culture of family, self-improvement and shared purpose.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is back: When and where to watch India A vs Sri Lanka AVaibhav Sooryavanshi returns as India A open the Dambulla tri-series against Sri Lanka A on Tuesday. Tilak Varma will lead a young Indian side in the tri-series, which will also feature Afghanistan A side.

What really happened at London nightclub Ben Stokes and England teammate visitedBen Stokes and Gus Atkinson are being investigated after a late-night altercation in London following England's win over New Zealand. The episode has delayed England's second-Test selection and reopened uneasy questions over leadership, discipline and control.

No Virat Kohli. Will Rohit Sharma play ODIs vs Afghanistan?India are awaiting medical clearance on Rohit Sharma for the Afghanistan ODI series. Hardik Pandya has been cleared, while Virat Kohli has been ruled out with a hamstring tear.

You're a gift to the sport: Andre Agassi hails Chwalinska's Roland Garros runAndre Agassi delivered an emotional message to Maja Chwalinska after her dream run to the Roland Garros final came to an end. The American legend hailed the Polish qualifier's historic achievement and called her "a gift to the sport."

Ben Stokes in trouble? England cricket probe 'nightclub incident' after Lord's TestEngland captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
